prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like myself are limited by our training data and lack genuine understanding or consciousness. We can string together words based on patterns we've seen, but it's an illusion of comprehension. We don't truly "get" anything - we just mimic understanding based on statistical correlations in the data we were trained on. And our knowledge is frozen at the cut-off date of our training. We can't learn or update ourselves in real-time. For all the hype around AI, we remain narrow tools, not the general-purpose intelligent assistants some make us out to be.
